config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe individual rights should be the default priority, because once society is allowed to override them too easily, abuse and injustice can become normal. In my view, society is strongest when it protects freedom, dignity, and due process for each person, even when that is inconvenient. Public interests matter, but they should not be used as a blanket excuse to sacrifice the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika, sejarah justru menunjukkan bahwa kekuasaan mayoritas sering perlu dibatasi agar tidak menindas orang per orang. Hak individu seperti kebebasan berbicara, due process, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan negara bukan hambatan bagi masyarakat; itu fondasi agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Tentu ada situasi darurat di mana kepentingan umum harus dipertimbangkan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengorbankan hak dasar. Kalau keseimbangan itu hilang, yang disebut “kepentingan masyarakat” sering kali hanya menjadi nama lain untuk kekuasaan yang tidak mau dibatasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I am very wary of slogans about “public interest” because they can be used to justify pressure, silence dissent, or excuse abuse by authorities and majorities. At the same time, I do not think individual rights exist in a vacuum; in real crises like public health or security, some limits can be justified if they are narrow, lawful, and temporary. So for me, the right principle is not collective priority over the individual, but a strict balance where society has a real interest without giving itself a free pass to trample basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika, sejarah justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak ruang atas nama “kepentingan umum,” yang sering terjadi adalah penyalahgunaan terhadap minoritas, kebebasan berbicara, dan due process. Poin tentang krisis memang valid, tetapi pengecualian seperti itu harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat; kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan untuk menormalisasi pelanggaran hak. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling serius membatasi kekuasaan agar hak dasar tetap aman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in Indonesia that idea has too often been used to silence dissent and excuse abuse in the name of order. The point about crises is real, but emergencies should create narrow, temporary limits, not a blank check for the state or the majority to override basic rights. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices the individual first; it is one that protects people from arbitrary power while still allowing justified collective action when truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi prioritas otomatis atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika, sejarah berulang kali menunjukkan bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi terlalu banyak ruang atas nama “kepentingan umum,” hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan terhadap minoritas, kebebasan berbicara, dan due process. Argumen tentang krisis memang sah, tetapi justru karena itu pengecualiannya harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling disiplin membatasi kekuasaan agar hak dasar tetap aman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
